title: Span Events and Links
description: SpanEvent, ExceptionSpanEvent, and SpanLink — structured annotations attached to spans in the DDTrace extension stub (DDTrace namespace).

classes.name: DDTrace\SpanEvent
classes.notes: name = event label; timestamp in nanoseconds (defaults to current Unix timestamp); attributes = key/value metadata bag

classes.name: DDTrace\ExceptionSpanEvent
classes.notes: Specialises SpanEvent to record PHP exceptions. Inherits name/attributes/timestamp from SpanEvent. Constructor auto-populates from the Throwable.

classes.name: DDTrace\SpanLink
attributes: public string $traceId   // 32-char lower-case hex; populate via SpanData::getLink()
public string $spanId    // 16-char lower-case hex; populate via SpanData::getLink()
classes.notes: fromHeaders() consumes W3C/Datadog distributed tracing headers to construct a link to an external trace. traceId/spanId should not be set directly from SpanData::$id — use SpanData::getLink() instead.

classes.name: DDTrace\SpanData
relevant_methods: getLink(): SpanLink   // returns pre-populated SpanLink with current span's traceId + spanId
classes.notes: SpanData owns both collections. Callers append SpanEvent/ExceptionSpanEvent to $events and SpanLink to $links.

key_relationships: SpanData::getLink() creates and returns a SpanLink (factory association)
SpanLink::fromHeaders() is a static factory producing SpanLink from HTTP headers

interface_boundary.JsonSerializable: Both SpanEvent and SpanLink implement \JsonSerializable, enabling direct JSON encoding for serialisation to the Datadog Agent payload.

domain_notes: SpanEvents are timestamped, named annotations with arbitrary key/value attributes — modelled after OpenTelemetry span events.
ExceptionSpanEvent is a convenience subclass that records exception type, message, and stack trace as a structured event.
SpanLinks cross-reference other traces/spans (causal or otherwise), supporting parallel/fan-out workflows and distributed context propagation.
traceId in SpanLink is 128-bit (32 hex chars) unlike SpanData::$id which is 64-bit — direct assignment would silently truncate; always use SpanData::getLink().
SpanLink::fromHeaders() supports both array-form and callback-form header consumption (same pattern as DDTrace distributed tracing APIs).
